summary: DHH describes his appearance on Danish TV show 16 1/2, where he spent seven hours filming a five-minute segment showcasing his tech gadgets. The segment featured his iPod, 3G phone, and PowerBook, but the real hook was the lifestyle they enabled rather than the devices themselves. The shoot took place outdoors at Charlottenlund Fortet, staged as if he'd moved his office outside to enjoy the weather. The post is a brief, casual update about the experience.
key_points: The TV segment focused on gadgets but found the cord-free, location-independent lifestyle more compelling than the devices themselves
DHH showcased Apple products (iPod, PowerBook) and a 3G phone as his essential tech kit in 2004
Seven hours of shooting yielded just five minutes of television, highlighting the production overhead of media
money_quotes: The gadgets in themselves doesn't seem to excite as much as the combination of gadgets and a life free from cords and location-fixed obligations.
key_insight: Even in 2004, the real appeal of technology was not the gadgets themselves but the location-independent lifestyle they enabled.
